recognized_artifact: Bloom's Taxonomy (cognitive domain) - hierarchy of educational/cognitive task levels
ambiguities: The source content describes a taxonomy/classification structure, not a procedural workflow. Forcing it into primitive_sequence/transitions/loops/branches fields imposes sequential and control-flow semantics that don't map cleanly onto a static hierarchy of categories.

'Classify' covers placing activities into levels, but the source also implies a ranked/ordinal relationship between levels (simpler vs. more complex), which the allowed primitive list doesn't distinguish from simple nominal Classify.

'Select or evaluate the intended level of challenge' could map to either Decide (selecting a level) or Evaluate (judging appropriateness) - both are plausible, so Evaluate was chosen as the closer fit but this is a judgment call.
No primitive in the allowed list captures 'ordering levels by complexity' (a ranking/stratifying operation) as distinct from categorical Classify - flagged in candidate_missing_primitives.
notes: This artifact is fundamentally a classification taxonomy (categories ordered by complexity), not a control-flow process. The JSON schema's procedural fields (transitions, loops, branches) are largely inapplicable and left empty/minimal to avoid inventing false structure.
